name: Browser Automation
purpose: Provides advanced, AI-driven DOM interaction, visual interpretation, and self-healing autonomous navigation capabilities.

safety,cost,resources.why: Per strict hierarchy rules, Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates a Cloud API. Hyperbrowser completely offloads the Chromium instance to the cloud, guaranteeing absolute isolation from the macOS host.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. Browser Use deployed within the OrbStack container provides the required execution boundary while utilizing efficient local APIs.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 forbids paid Cloud APIs. With Safety as the secondary priority, Browser Use is Dockerized to ensure free local automation without exposing the bare-metal host.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 forbids paid Cloud APIs. With Resources prioritized over Safety, the Rust-native AgentBrowser is executed bare-metal to completely bypass Docker virtualization overhead.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 strictly mandates a Cloud API. Hyperbrowser ensures zero local compute is consumed, preserving all host memory.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates a highly optimized native binary. AgentBrowser runs bare-metal to deliver accessibility trees with absolute minimal CPU/RAM impact.

safety,cost,resources.why: Per strict hierarchy rules, Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates a Cloud API. Hyperbrowser offloads execution, ensuring no rogue navigation flows escape into the Windows host.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. Browser Use leverages WSL2's highly efficient containerization to isolate the agent from the Windows filesystem.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 demands a free, locally hosted solution. Safety #2 dictates Dockerization, making WSL2-hosted Browser Use the optimal balance of zero financial expenditure and secure containment.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 demands a free solution. Resources #2 dictates bypassing WSL2 overhead where possible. AgentBrowser is executed bare-metal for maximum asynchronous speed on a constrained machine.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 strictly mandates a Cloud API. This eliminates all local Chromium overhead, keeping the Windows host highly responsive.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates highly optimized native execution. AgentBrowser's Rust implementation is run bare-metal to maximize efficiency.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates a Cloud API. Hyperbrowser isolates external web interactions entirely from the dedicated monolith, ensuring core database services cannot be compromised.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. Stagehand provides containerized isolation, while its caching mechanism is critical for optimizing the 24/7 token efficiency of the monolith.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 forbids paid APIs. Stagehand's action-caching drastically reduces recurring LLM token spend for 24/7 background tasks, while Dockerization satisfies the Safety #2 requirement.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 forbids paid APIs. With Resources #2 over Safety, AgentBrowser is executed bare-metal to eliminate Docker network overhead and preserve maximum CPU limits.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 strictly mandates a Cloud API. This preserves the 32GB+ monolith's CPU/RAM entirely for VictoriaLogs and PostgreSQL vector indexing.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 strictly mandates a highly optimized native binary. AgentBrowser runs bare-metal to ensure the background daemon consumes almost zero continuous resources.

safety,cost,resources.why: Because this is Tier > 2, Cloud APIs are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN. Safety #1 forbids bare-metal. Therefore, Skyvern runs in a Dockerized environment securely containing its heavy vision-processing loops.

safety,resources,cost.why: Because this is Tier > 2, Cloud APIs are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN. Safety #1 forbids bare-metal. Therefore, Skyvern runs in a Dockerized environment securely containing its heavy vision-processing loops.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 demands a free local solution. Safety #2 dictates Dockerization. Skyvern containerizes the blast radius of heavy visual inference tasks and erratic AI DOM scraping on the execution node.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 demands a free solution. Resources #2 dictates bare-metal execution to bypass containerization bottlenecks. AgentBrowser runs natively to leverage unhindered access to the blast-zone CPU.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 demands minimal host impact. Because Tier > 2 strictly forbids Cloud APIs, we must fallback to highly optimized native binaries. AgentBrowser runs bare-metal to prioritize hardware efficiency.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 demands minimal host impact. Because Tier > 2 strictly forbids Cloud APIs, we must fallback to highly optimized native binaries. AgentBrowser runs bare-metal to prioritize hardware efficiency.
